summary: A compound inequality combines two conditions with "and" (the solution is the overlap) or "or" (the solution is the union).

formulas.name: Combined (sandwich) form
formulas.expression: a < x + b < c \implies a - b < x < c - b

explanation: An "and" compound inequality (like \(1 < x < 5\)) requires both conditions to hold, so the solution is the overlap of the two ranges. An "or" compound inequality (like \(x < 1\) or \(x > 5\)) requires at least one condition to hold, so the solution is everything outside a middle range. To solve a "sandwich" inequality like \(3 < 2x+1 < 9\), perform the same operation on all three parts simultaneously: subtract 1 from all three parts, then divide all three by 2. As always, dividing all three parts by a negative number flips both inequality signs.
